platform.name: mobile-maintenance-platform
platform.role: mobile-maintenance solution wrapper and governance repository
platform.description: Owns epics, stories, architecture, ADRs, agent governance, and Copilot workspace configuration for the maintenance mobile solution. It does not contain product implementation code by default.

mms-app.description: Implements maintenance-specific mobile app features and user workflows.
agentGuidance: Use this repository as the primary implementation target when the issue belongs to the service mobile app.
Before creating new UI or app logic, inspect shared dependencies for reusable capabilities.
Create follow-up tasks if required changes belong to shared repositories.

maui-toolkit.description: Provides reusable MAUI controls, styles, themes, navigation helpers, and UI patterns.
agentGuidance: Reuse existing controls, styles, and patterns before creating app-local UI components.
Do not introduce dependencies from this repository to concrete mobile app repositories.

net-client-api.role: .NET backend client API, contracts, DTOs, and service access repository
net-client-api.description: Source of truth for backend API clients, DTOs, and contracts used by mobile repositories.
agentGuidance: Do not duplicate API contracts in consuming repositories.
Higher-level repositories may depend on this repository, but this repository must not depend on app or UI toolkit repositories.

rules.platformRepositoryIsWrapper: The platform repository owns epics, stories, architecture, governance, and workspace configuration. Product implementation belongs to code repositories.
rules.issueHierarchy: Epics and stories should live on the platform repository. Implementation tasks should live in the affected code repositories.
rules.primaryRepositoryOnly: Implement changes only in the code repository that owns the issue or pull request unless explicitly instructed otherwise.
rules.dependencyGraphIsAuthoritative: Use the configured dependency graph as the source of truth for allowed dependency directions and workspace selection.
rules.projectReferenceLayout: Code repositories are checked out as sibling directories below the platform workspace root. Relative .NET ProjectReference paths should be based on this layout.
rules.reuseBeforeCreate: Search checked-out dependency repositories for reusable assets, APIs, contracts, components, controls, sync capabilities, or patterns before creating new functionality.
rules.crossRepoChanges: If a change is required in another code repository, describe the required follow-up task instead of silently modifying that repository.
